text: Strength training improves running economy by 2-8% in middle- and long-distance runners compared to running-only controls.
population: middle- and long-distance runners with ≥6 months experience

text: Strength training improves time trial performance over 1.5-10 km distances in middle- and long-distance runners.
population: middle- and long-distance runners with ≥6 months experience

text: Strength training does not negatively impact body composition in distance runners, alleviating concerns about unwanted weight gain.
population: middle- and long-distance runners with ≥6 months experience

text: VO2max, velocity at VO2max, and blood lactate markers are typically unaffected by the addition of strength training to a running program.
population: middle- and long-distance runners with ≥6 months experience

text: Anaerobic speed qualities tend to improve following strength training in distance runners.
population: middle- and long-distance runners with ≥6 months experience

text: Two to three strength training sessions per week incorporating a variety of modalities (heavy resistance, explosive resistance, and plyometric training) are recommended for middle- and long-distance runners.
population: middle- and long-distance runners with ≥6 months experience

text: Strength training interventions of at least 4 weeks duration are needed to produce measurable physiological benefits in distance runners.
population: middle- and long-distance runners with ≥6 months experience
